roadmap.scope_note: CONTINUATION PASS: slices S0, S1, S2 are DELIVERED (phases 00/01/02 — do not re-execute). This pass ADDS S3, S4, S5, S6 to IN scope. S6 (unsupported-construct rejection, phase 06) is a reject-list independent of supported codegen and lands FIRST in dependency terms (DDD-8) so nothing slips through silently while S3/S4 develop. S3 (choices/gathers/weave, phase 03) is the research-flagged HIGHEST-RISK slice — its 4-fixture weave corpus IS the spike gate (DWD-6/ADR-008). S4 (supported ceiling: conditionals/functions/tunnels/ref-params/tags, phase 04) is a large parser extension lowering to already-implemented runtime NodeKind cases, validated end-to-end by native compile of TheIntercept.ink. S5 (feature-reference consistency, phase 05) goes executable-GREEN automatically once S3/S4/S6 land and additionally delivers the prose feature-reference document. Each step flips its named DISTILL-authored acceptance scenario(s) GREEN and removes the CompileErrorKind.scaffold sentinel for that slice; the RED acceptance suites are flipped GREEN, never modified. [DELIVER fold 2026-06-14: step 03-02 (D6 choice-flag/invisible-default encoding) folded into 03-01 - S3 oracle corpus validates the encoding per DDD-9 and was 4/4 GREEN after 03-01; conditional-choice/read-count lowering is driven RED by S4 TheIntercept e2e. No orphaned work.] [DELIVER descope 2026-06-14 (user-approved): step 04-03 (TheIntercept e2e) removed - TheIntercept.ink line 86 uses a variable-text sequence outside the locked supported set (rejected by S6/DDD-12). Documented compiler/runtime parity gap; the supported ceiling is validated by the two compile-ceiling S4 tests. 05-01 dep repointed 04-03->04-02.]

phases.name: S0 — Walking Skeleton (compile and play one line)

steps.name: No-JSON blueprint path + minimal plain-text compile-and-play
steps.description: Wire the read -> parse -> codegen -> runnable-story spine for a single line of plain text, with NO JSON round-trip. EXTEND StoryBlueprint with an internal init(root: ContainerNode) (DDD-2) so the compiler can hand a node tree straight to the runtime. CommentEliminator runs as a near pass-through (no comments in skeleton fixtures). A trivial line parse produces a flat AST; RuntimeObjectEmitter emits a root ContainerNode whose children are .text(line) + .newline per source line, terminated by .controlCommand("done"). InkCompiler.compile(source:) wires the pipeline and stops throwing .scaffold for plain-text input; Story(inkSource:) convenience delegates to it. Empty source compiles to a root that emits no text and ends cleanly. Compiler/ constructs Decoder/ node types only — it must NOT import Engine/ (R5), and uses no JSONSerialization (R3).
acceptance_criteria: A one-line plain-text source (compile-skeleton-hello fixture, 'Hello, world.') compiles in-process via InkCompiler.compile(source:) and plays through the runtime, emitting exactly the oracle's line with no external inklecate process invoked
The convenience surface Story(inkSource:) yields a playable Story directly from source string without a JSON intermediate
An empty source compiles to a Story that ends cleanly (canContinue false, no emitted text), matching the empty-source oracle
StoryBlueprint exposes an internal init(root: ContainerNode) that the runtime consumes via the existing Story(blueprint:) path
CompileErrorKind.scaffold is no longer thrown for plain-text or empty source
steps.scenario_name: a one-line plain-text story compiles in-process and plays, matching the oracle; the convenience compile surface yields a playable story from source; an empty source compiles to a story that ends cleanly, matching the oracle
quality_gates: 3 of 4 S0 scenarios GREEN (play, convenience, empty-source)
All pre-existing runtime suites remain GREEN (no regression — 233 baseline)
SwiftLint R5 boundary gate passes (Compiler/ does not import Engine/)
SwiftLint R3 gate passes (no JSONSerialization in Compiler/)
Compiler_NoInklecateGuardrailTests stays GREEN (no Process/inklecate in Compiler/)
implementation_notes: EXTEND Facade/StoryBlueprint.swift: add `internal init(root: ContainerNode)` alongside the existing `public init(json:)` — this is the no-JSON seam (DDD-2)
RuntimeObjectEmitter emits root ContainerNode: per non-empty line -> [.text(line), .newline]; append .controlCommand("done") to terminate. Empty source -> root with only .controlCommand("done")
CommentEliminator is a pass-through for S0 fixtures (strip // and /* */ implemented now; no comments exercised until later slices) — keep the pass minimal but real
StringParser may be a trivial line splitter at S0; the full stateful cursor/combinator engine lands in 01-01
InkCompiler.compile(source:) wires CommentEliminator -> parse -> RuntimeObjectEmitter -> StoryBlueprint(root:); remove the .scaffold throw for the plain-text path
Story(inkSource:) convenience already scaffolded in Compiler/InkCompiler.swift — delegate to compile(source:) then Story(blueprint:)
BOUNDARY: Compiler/ may construct Decoder/ ContainerNode + NodeKind but must NOT import Engine/ (TreeWalker/InkEngine/StoryState) — R5 enforced
BOUNDARY: do NOT call JSONSerialization anywhere in Compiler/ — R3 enforced
Tests use Swift Testing backtick function names (project mandate); mutation testing is disabled (project-wide)

TEST PARADIGM (project): paradigm is OBJECT-ORIENTED / example-based (CLAUDE.md). Unit tests are example-based using Swift Testing backtick function names. Property-based / state-machine PBT is explicitly OUT (DISTILL DWD-9); the correctness instrument is the execution-equivalence oracle suite plus targeted example unit tests.
STAGE CONTEXT (cascade): NEW code = Stage 0 (paradigm-from-day-zero, no debt accumulates).

EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM (acceptance level): the three S0 acceptance scenarios are execution-equivalence golden-oracle diffs (line-list compared to a committed inklecate .ink.json played through the same runtime) — single-example by nature. Compensate with property-based tests at unit level on the RuntimeObjectEmitter line->node mapping (universe: child-node sequence vs source-line list).
criteria: A one-line plain-text source (compile-skeleton-hello fixture, 'Hello, world.') compiles in-process via InkCompiler.compile(source:) and plays through the runtime, emitting exactly the oracle's line with no external inklecate process invoked
The convenience surface Story(inkSource:) yields a playable Story directly from source string without a JSON intermediate
An empty source compiles to a Story that ends cleanly (canContinue false, no emitted text), matching the empty-source oracle
StoryBlueprint exposes an internal init(root: ContainerNode) that the runtime consumes via the existing Story(blueprint:) path
CompileErrorKind.scaffold is no longer thrown for plain-text or empty source

steps.name: Secondary Ink-JSON sink (emitJSON) without JSONSerialization
steps.description: Add the D4 secondary output sink: InkCompiler.emitJSON for a supported source produces Ink-JSON text. The JSON emitter walks the same ContainerNode tree produced for the compile path and serialises it to Ink-JSON. CONSTRAINT (R3/R5): it must NOT use JSONSerialization — use JSONEncoder and/or deterministic string building. The emitted JSON must contain the Ink version header key ("inkVersion") and the literal text payload ("Hello, world.") so the round-trippable artifact is recognisable as Ink-JSON.
acceptance_criteria: InkCompiler.emitJSON for the compile-skeleton-hello source returns a string that contains "inkVersion"
The emitted JSON string contains the source text payload "Hello, world."
emitJSON produces its output without invoking JSONSerialization (R3) and without importing Engine/ (R5)
CompileErrorKind.scaffold is no longer thrown by emitJSON for supported source
steps.scenario_name: the secondary JSON sink emits Ink-JSON for a supported source
quality_gates: 4 of 4 S0 scenarios GREEN (S0 suite fully GREEN)
All pre-existing runtime suites remain GREEN (no regression)
SwiftLint R3 gate passes (JSONEmitter uses JSONEncoder/string building, never JSONSerialization)
SwiftLint R5 boundary gate passes (no Engine/ import)
Compiler_NoInklecateGuardrailTests stays GREEN
implementation_notes: NEW Compiler/Codegen/JSONEmitter.swift: consume the ContainerNode tree from RuntimeObjectEmitter (00-01) and serialise to Ink-JSON
MUST emit the top-level "inkVersion" key and serialise text nodes as Ink-JSON string entries (the literal 'Hello, world.' must appear)

R3 CONSTRAINT: do NOT use JSONSerialization — use Foundation.JSONEncoder with Encodable model types, or deterministic manual string building. SwiftLint R3 custom rule is path-scoped to Compiler/ and will fail the build otherwise
InkCompiler.emitJSON delegates to JSONEmitter; remove the .scaffold throw on the emitJSON path
This sink is secondary (D4): the primary compile path (00-01) does NOT depend on it — emitJSON is a separate entry point, not part of compile(source:)
BOUNDARY: JSONEmitter constructs/reads Decoder/ node types only; must NOT import Engine/ (R5)

phases.name: S1 — Core Flow (knots, stitches, diverts, glue)

steps.name: Parser core: stateful StringParser + comment elimination + line/statement scanning
steps.description: Build the real parser substrate the S1/S2 statement rules depend on. CommentEliminator strips // line comments and /* */ block comments before parsing. StringParser is a stateful cursor that tracks line/column and provides parser combinators. InkParser scans the source into a typed AST (Compiler/AST/) carrying source positions, recognising knot headers (=== name ===), stitch headers (= name), divert statements (-> target, including qualified target.stitch and relative .^.stitch as unresolved path strings), glue tokens (<>), and the -> END terminator, plus plain text lines. At this step the AST is produced and positions are attached; divert path RESOLUTION and codegen happen in 01-02. No Engine/ import; no JSONSerialization.
acceptance_criteria: CommentEliminator removes // line comments and /* */ block comments while preserving line numbering for downstream positions
StringParser tracks line and column as it consumes the source and exposes combinators usable by the statement rules
InkParser produces a typed AST node for each of: knot header, stitch header, divert (absolute, qualified, relative .^), glue, -> END, and plain text line — each carrying its source position
Relative (.^.stitch) and qualified (knot.stitch) divert targets are captured as unresolved path strings on the AST for resolution in the next step
Parsing the compile-linear-flow fixture yields an AST that round-trips its knot/stitch/divert/glue structure with no .scaffold thrown during parse
steps.scenario_name: (enabling step — no S1 acceptance scenario flips GREEN until 01-02 codegen lands; validated via the AST/parser unit suite this step introduces)
quality_gates: Parser/AST unit tests GREEN for knot/stitch/divert/glue/END/text recognition with positions
S1 acceptance suite remains RED for the right reason (codegen not yet wired — 01-02 flips it)
All pre-existing runtime suites remain GREEN (no regression)
SwiftLint R5 (no Engine/ import) and R3 (no JSONSerialization) gates pass in Compiler/
implementation_notes: NEW Compiler/Lexer/CommentEliminator.swift: strip // to end-of-line and /* */ (possibly multi-line); preserve newline count so AST positions stay accurate
NEW Compiler/Parser/StringParser.swift: stateful cursor with line/col tracking + combinator primitives (peek, advance, match, optional, repeat)

NEW Compiler/Parser/InkParser.swift (and InkParser*.swift split files as needed): statement rules for knot header (=== name ===), stitch header (= name), divert (-> target), glue (<>), -> END, plain text

NEW Compiler/AST/*.swift: typed parsed-AST node kinds with source positions and unresolved-path fields for diverts (store target as raw string: absolute 'intro', qualified 'investigation.arrival', relative '.^.arrival')
Path RESOLUTION (mapping unresolved strings to namedContent paths) is deliberately deferred to 01-02 codegen — this step only parses and captures
BOUNDARY: Compiler/ must NOT import Engine/ (R5); must NOT use JSONSerialization (R3)
files_to_modify lists 3 NEW production files (StringParser, InkParser, AST) — CommentEliminator counted in the deliverables/scope; ratio stays <= 2.5
Tests use Swift Testing backtick function names; mutation testing disabled

criteria: CommentEliminator removes // line comments and /* */ block comments while preserving line numbering for downstream positions
StringParser tracks line and column as it consumes the source and exposes combinators usable by the statement rules
InkParser produces a typed AST node for each of: knot header, stitch header, divert (absolute, qualified, relative .^), glue, -> END, and plain text line — each carrying its source position
Relative (.^.stitch) and qualified (knot.stitch) divert targets are captured as unresolved path strings on the AST for resolution in the next step
Parsing the compile-linear-flow fixture yields an AST that round-trips its knot/stitch/divert/glue structure with no .scaffold thrown during parse

steps.name: Core-flow codegen: named content, divert path resolution, glue
steps.description: Extend RuntimeObjectEmitter to lower the S1 AST into a ContainerNode tree that PLAYS identically to the oracle. Knots and stitches become entries in namedContent (stitches nested under their parent knot's container). Diverts emit .divert(target,...) with targets resolved from the unresolved path strings: absolute (-> intro), qualified (-> investigation.arrival), and relative (.^.arrival) all map to the correct namedContent path. Glue (<>) joins adjacent lines so output matches the oracle exactly. -> END terminates. This flips the two S1 scenarios GREEN. Codegen has full freedom over tree shape — only PLAY-equivalence to the committed inklecate oracle is required, not structural-JSON equivalence.
acceptance_criteria: The multi-knot linear story (compile-linear-flow fixture: knots, a stitch, absolute/qualified/relative diverts, glue, -> END) compiles and plays, with the emitted line list identical to the inklecate oracle played through the same runtime
Glue (<>) joins lines exactly as the oracle produces them (no spurious newline between glued segments)
Absolute (-> intro), qualified (-> investigation.arrival), and relative (-> .^.arrival) diverts all resolve to the correct namedContent target and route execution as the oracle does
Knots and stitches are emitted as namedContent entries reachable by their resolved paths
CompileErrorKind.scaffold is no longer thrown for S1 core-flow source
steps.scenario_name: a multi-knot linear story compiles and plays, matching the oracle; glue joins lines exactly as the oracle produces them
quality_gates: 2 of 2 S1 scenarios GREEN (S1 suite fully GREEN)
All S0 scenarios remain GREEN (no regression)
All pre-existing runtime suites remain GREEN
SwiftLint R5 and R3 gates pass in Compiler/

implementation_notes: EXTEND Compiler/Codegen/RuntimeObjectEmitter.swift: lower knot/stitch AST nodes into ContainerNode.namedContent (stitch container nested in its knot container, with the .name set)

Divert resolution: map unresolved path strings to namedContent paths — absolute -> top-level named container; qualified knot.stitch -> nested; relative .^.stitch -> sibling-relative resolution. Emit .divert(target:, isConditional:false, isVariable:false)
Glue: emit so the runtime joins segments without an intervening newline — match the oracle's exact line list (execution-equivalence, NOT structural-JSON equivalence)
-> END lowers to the runtime's end control (.controlCommand("end") or the divert-to-END convention the runtime already recognises) — confirm against how the Decoder/runtime represents END
Codegen is free to emit ANY ContainerNode tree that PLAYS identically — do not chase byte-for-byte JSON parity with inklecate
Compiler/Error/CompileError.swift: remove the .scaffold usage on the S1 path (the case stays for S3-S6 which are out of scope here)
BOUNDARY: no Engine/ import (R5); no JSONSerialization (R3)

phases.name: S2 — Variables & Expressions (VAR, CONST, temp, operators)

steps.name: Pratt expression sub-parser + arithmetic evaluation codegen
steps.description: Add a Pratt (precedence-climbing) expression sub-parser to InkParser for arithmetic and operator expressions, producing typed expression AST nodes. Extend RuntimeObjectEmitter to lower expressions into the runtime's evaluation form: .controlCommand("ev") / .controlCommand("/ev") evaluation brackets wrapping .intValue/.floatValue operands and .nativeFunction("+"|"*"|...) operations, with .controlCommand("out") to emit the computed value. Correct operator precedence (e.g. 2 + 3 * 4 == 14) must render identically to the oracle. This is the expression substrate the declaration/interpolation step (02-02) builds on.
acceptance_criteria: The Pratt sub-parser parses arithmetic expressions with correct precedence (multiplication binds tighter than addition) into a typed expression AST
RuntimeObjectEmitter lowers an inline arithmetic expression {2 + 3 * 4} into ev/native-function/out runtime nodes that evaluate to 14 when played
UNIT INVARIANT: a played inline integer arithmetic expression {2 + 3 * 4} yields 14 (precedence correct) — full S2 scenario GREEN is deferred to 02-02
Integer and float literal operands lower to .intValue/.floatValue and operators to .nativeFunction with the correct symbol
steps.scenario_name: (enabling step — no S2 acceptance scenario flips GREEN until 02-02 codegen lands; validated via the Pratt expression-parser unit suite this step introduces)
quality_gates: Pratt expression-parser unit tests GREEN (precedence, associativity, literal/operator lowering)
Arithmetic evaluation plays to the correct value through the runtime
All S0 and S1 scenarios remain GREEN (no regression)
All pre-existing runtime suites remain GREEN
SwiftLint R5 and R3 gates pass in Compiler/

EXTEND Compiler/Codegen/RuntimeObjectEmitter.swift: lower expression AST to .controlCommand("ev") ... operands/.nativeFunction(op) ... .controlCommand("/ev") then .controlCommand("out") for inline-printed expressions
Literals: integer -> .intValue, float -> .floatValue; operators '+','*', etc. -> .nativeFunction(symbol)
Confirm the runtime's evaluation-stack ordering (postfix/RPN-style) the Decoder/runtime expects, and emit operands/operators in that order so the play result matches the oracle

steps.name: VAR/CONST/temp declarations, CONST inlining, variable read/assignment, string interpolation
steps.description: Complete S2: parse and lower VAR globals (VAR name = "Ada", VAR score = 3), CONST (CONST BONUS = 10) with compile-time inlining, and temp declarations (~ temp total = score + BONUS). Emit .variableAssignment(name,isGlobal) for declarations/assignments and .variableReference(name) for reads. CONST references are inlined to their literal value at codegen (no runtime variable). String interpolation {name}, {score}, {total} lowers to ev/variable-reference/out (with str/.str brackets for string output). Combined with 02-01's arithmetic, this flips both S2 scenarios GREEN, with all values rendering identically to the oracle (Total: 13, Math: 14).
acceptance_criteria: The state-driven compile-variables fixture (VAR name/score, CONST BONUS, ~ temp total = score + BONUS, interpolations, arithmetic, -> END) compiles and plays with the emitted line list identical to the inklecate oracle
CONST BONUS is inlined to its literal value at codegen (BONUS does not exist as a runtime variable) and arithmetic renders values identical to the oracle (total = score + 10 = 13; 2 + 3 * 4 = 14)
Global VAR declarations lower to .variableAssignment(name, isGlobal:true) and temp declarations to .variableAssignment(name, isGlobal:false)
Variable reads ({name}, {score}, {total}) lower to .variableReference(name) inside ev/out (string interpolation) and render the stored value, with strings wrapped in str/.str output brackets
CompileErrorKind.scaffold is no longer thrown for S2 variables-and-expressions source

phases.name: S6 — Unsupported-Construct Rejection (named, located, no story)

steps.name: Lexer-level reject-list: variable-text sequences (sequence/cycle/once/shuffle)
steps.description: Detect the four variable-text alternative constructs during the lexer/inline scan and reject each with a NAMED + LOCATED error and NO story produced. The four constructs share the {...|...} alternative syntax and are distinguished by their leading marker: plain sequence {a|b|c} (construct 'sequence'), cycle {&a|b} (construct 'cycle'), once {!a|b} (construct 'once'), shuffle {~a|b} (construct 'shuffle'). On encountering any of these during parse/scan, throw CompileError(kind:.unsupportedConstruct, construct:<name>, line:>0, column:) where the construct substring is matched case-insensitively (sequence/cycle/once/shuffle). These fixtures currently throw .scaffold or .syntaxError; after this step they throw the located .unsupportedConstruct error and produce no Story. Covers Feature Coverage Matrix rows 25-28 and the reject half of S5 for these four constructs.
acceptance_criteria: The reject-seq fixture (variable-text sequence {a|b|c}) is rejected with CompileError(kind:.unsupportedConstruct) whose construct field contains 'sequence' (case-insensitive), with line > 0 and a column, and NO Story is produced
The reject-cycle fixture ({&a|b}) is rejected with construct containing 'cycle', line > 0, no Story
The reject-once fixture ({!a|b}) is rejected with construct containing 'once', line > 0, no Story
The reject-shuffle fixture ({~a|b}) is rejected with construct containing 'shuffle', line > 0, no Story
No partial or empty Story is emitted for any rejected source — rejection is fail-closed (the throw precedes any StoryBlueprint construction)
steps.scenario_name: a variable-text alternative construct (sequence/cycle/once/shuffle) is rejected with a named, located error and no story
quality_gates: 4 of 8 S6 reject scenarios GREEN (sequence/cycle/once/shuffle)

All pre-existing runtime suites remain GREEN

implementation_notes: Detect the {marker...|...} alternative forms at the inline/lexer scan: plain {a|b} -> 'sequence'; {&...} -> 'cycle'; {!...} -> 'once'; {~...} -> 'shuffle'. Distinguish from conditionals/interpolation {x} (no top-level | with these markers) so S2/S4 supported forms are NOT mis-rejected
Throw CompileError(kind:.unsupportedConstruct, construct:<name>, line:>0, column:) BEFORE any StoryBlueprint is built — rejection must be fail-closed (no partial story)
construct field must contain the required substring case-insensitively; the S5/S6 suites match on that substring
NEW Compiler/Parser/UnsupportedConstructDetector.swift if the detection logic is non-trivial; fold into InkParser.swift only if it stays small (keep steps/production-files ratio <= 2.5)
EXTEND Compiler/Error/CompileError.swift: ensure .unsupportedConstruct carries construct/line/column (add fields if the existing case lacks them)

steps.name: Statement-level reject-list: thread / LIST / RANDOM / EXTERNAL
steps.description: Detect the four statement-level unsupported constructs and reject each with a NAMED + LOCATED error and NO story produced. Constructs and required construct-name substring (case-insensitive): thread <- knot (construct 'thread'); LIST x = a,b (construct 'list'); RANDOM(...) / SEED_RANDOM(...) (construct 'random'); EXTERNAL f() (construct 'external'). On detection during the statement scan, throw CompileError(kind:.unsupportedConstruct, construct:<name>, line:>0, column:). Fixtures reject-thread/list/random/external currently throw .scaffold or .syntaxError; after this step they throw the located .unsupportedConstruct error and produce no Story. Covers Feature Coverage Matrix rows 36-39 and the reject half of S5 for these four constructs.

phases.name: S3 — Choices & Gathers / Weave (HIGHEST-RISK, weave-spike gate)

steps.name: General weave resolver: nested weaves, labeled/multiple gathers, plain-label + sticky choices, sealed weaves
steps.description: EXTEND the existing flat-only WeaveEmitter into a general weave resolver. The walking-skeleton WeaveEmitter currently PLAYS flat weaves but throws .scaffold for nested weaves, sticky '+' choices, plain-label choices, labeled gathers, and multiple gathers. This step resolves the full weave structure: indentation maps to choice/gather hierarchy (multi-level/nested weaves); labeled gathers (- (label)) and multiple gathers within a weave; plain-label choices and sticky '+' choices; gather loose-end stitching (content after a choice falls through to the next gather at the same or lower depth); and sealed weaves (every option diverts away, no fall-through gather). The fixtures compile-weave-nested, compile-weave-labeled-gather, and compile-weave-sealed must play choice-for-choice identical to the oracle along the suite's choice scripts. Codegen has full freedom over tree shape — only PLAY/choice equivalence to the committed inklecate oracle is required.
acceptance_criteria: The compile-weave-nested fixture (multi-level indented weave with nested choices and gathers) compiles and plays choice-for-choice identical to the inklecate oracle along the suite's choice script
The compile-weave-labeled-gather fixture (labeled gathers and multiple gathers) compiles and plays identical to the oracle, with gather loose-ends stitched as the oracle stitches them
The compile-weave-sealed fixture (all options divert away, no fall-through gather) compiles and plays identical to the oracle
Plain-label choices and sticky '+' choices are emitted so repeat/visibility behaviour matches the oracle along the choice script
CompileErrorKind.scaffold is no longer thrown for nested weaves, sticky choices, plain-label choices, labeled gathers, or multiple gathers
steps.scenario_name: a weave fixture compiles and plays choice-for-choice identical to the oracle (nested/labeled-gather/sealed)
quality_gates: compile-weave-flat remains GREEN (no regression of the walking-skeleton weave) and nested/labeled-gather/sealed fixtures GREEN

All pre-existing runtime suites remain GREEN

Compiler_NoInklecateGuardrailTests stays GREEN
implementation_notes: EXTEND Sources/SwiftInkRuntime/Compiler/Codegen/WeaveEmitter.swift — do NOT create a parallel emitter; build the general resolver on top of the existing flat path
Indentation -> hierarchy: track nesting depth so nested choices/gathers form the correct container tree; a gather at depth N collects loose ends from choices at depth > N

Gather loose-end stitching: content after a choice that does not divert falls through to the next gather at the same-or-lower depth; sealed weave = every option diverts away, so no fall-through gather is emitted
Plain-label choices (* [text] / * label) and sticky '+' choices: emit so the runtime's once-only vs sticky visibility matches the oracle along the choice script
EXTEND Compiler/Parser/InkParser.swift to parse the weave block structure (depth, labels, gathers) and Compiler/AST/CompilerAST.swift for the weave/choice/gather AST nodes
Codegen is free to emit ANY ContainerNode tree that PLAYS choice-for-choice identically — do not chase byte-for-byte JSON parity with inklecate
Depends on 06-01 for reject-safety: unsupported variable-text inside choice/gather content is already rejected, so the weave resolver need not guard those forms

phases.name: S4 — Supported Ceiling (conditionals, functions, tunnels, ref-params, tags)

steps.name: Conditionals (inline / block / switch) + tags
steps.description: Extend the parser and codegen for the conditional family and tags, lowering to runtime NodeKind cases the runtime already PLAYS (Feature Coverage Matrix rows 22-24,29-35 are IMPLEMENTED in the runtime — this is PARSER+CODEGEN lowering only). Inline conditionals {c:a|b}, block conditionals {c: ... - else: ...}, and switch conditionals {v: -1: ... -2: ...} all lower onto the existing isConditional divert pathway plus the ev/expression evaluation from 02-01. Tags #tag lower to the runtime's tag node. The parser currently fails with .unexpectedToken("raise(force)") and .unexpectedEndOfInput on these forms; after this step it parses and lowers them so they play identical to the oracle.

steps.name: Functions + inline calls, tunnels, reference parameters
steps.description: Extend parser and codegen for the function/tunnel/ref-param family, lowering to runtime NodeKind cases the runtime already PLAYS. Function definitions === f(params) === and inline calls {f()} lower to a {"f()":path} divert with a return address. Tunnels -> k -> lower to the runtime's tunnelDivert with its return convention. Reference parameters ref x lower to a variablePointer so mutations inside the function/tunnel propagate to the caller's variable, matching the oracle. This completes the supported-ceiling codegen ahead of the TheIntercept end-to-end validation in 04-03.
acceptance_criteria: A function definition === f(params) === with an inline call {f()} lowers to a divert-with-return-address and plays identical to the oracle (correct return point and printed value)
A tunnel -> k -> lowers to the runtime's tunnelDivert and returns to the call site as the oracle does
A reference parameter ref x lowers to a variablePointer so a mutation inside the function/tunnel is visible to the caller, matching the oracle
Inline function calls, tunnels, and ref-param mutation all play identical to the oracle
CompileErrorKind.scaffold is no longer thrown for function/tunnel/ref-param source
